Package algatorgraph

Class Graph<V,​W>

  • java.lang.Object
    • algatorgraph.Graph<V,​W>
  • Type Parameters:
    V - the type of vertices
    W - the type of weights on edges
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​Edge<V,​W>>
    public class Graph<V,​W>
extends java.lang.Object
implements org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​Edge<V,​W>>, java.io.Serializable
    Represents an undirected graph using the adjacency list representation. The graph supports generic vertex and edge types, with edges having a generic weight type. The adjacency lists are stored in a HashMap objects.

    Example usage:

    
 // Create a graph with String vertices and Integer weights
 Graph<String, Integer> cityGraph = new Graph<>();
 
 // Add vertices (cities)
 cityGraph.addVertex("New York");
 cityGraph.addVertex("Boston");
 
 // Add a weighted edge (distance between cities)
 cityGraph.addEdge("New York", "Boston", new Edge<>("New York", "Boston", 215));
 
 // Get all edges
 Set<Edge<String, Integer>> allRoutes = cityGraph.edgeSet();
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from interface org.jgrapht.Graph

        DEFAULT_EDGE_WEIGHT

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      Graph()
      Constructor creates an empty adjacencyList

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      Edge<V,​W> addEdge​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Adds an edge connecting the specified vertices, creating and returning a new edge.
      boolean addEdge​(V vertex1, V vertex2, Edge<V,​W> edge)
      Adds the specified edge to the graph, connecting the specified vertices.
      V addVertex()  
      boolean addVertex​(V vertex)
      Adds the specified vertex to the graph.
      boolean containsEdge​(Edge<V,​W> edge)
      Checks if the graph contains the specified edge.
      boolean containsEdge​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Checks if the graph contains an edge connecting the specified vertices.
      boolean containsVertex​(V vertex)
      Checks if the graph contains the specified vertex.
      int degreeOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the degree of the specified vertex, which is the total number of edges incident to the vertex.
      java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> edgeSet()
      Returns the set of all edges in the graph.
      java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> edgesOf​(V v)
      Returns the set of edges connected to the specified vertex.
      boolean equals​(java.lang.Object obj)
      Compares this undirected graph to the specified object for equality.
      java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> getAllEdges​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Returns all edges connecting the specified vertices.
      Edge<V,​W> getEdge​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Returns the edge connecting the specified vertices.
      V getEdgeSource​(Edge<V,​W> edge)
      Returns the source vertex of the specified edge.
      java.util.function.Supplier<Edge<V,​W>> getEdgeSupplier()
      Not implemented.
      V getEdgeTarget​(Edge<V,​W> edge)
      Returns the target vertex of the specified edge.
      double getEdgeWeight​(Edge<V,​W> edge)
      Returns the weight of the specified edge.
      org.jgrapht.GraphType getType()
      Returns the type of this graph.
      java.util.function.Supplier<V> getVertexSupplier()
      Not implemented.
      java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> incomingEdgesOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the set of edges incoming to the specified vertex.
      int inDegreeOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the in-degree of the specified vertex, which is the number of incoming edges to the vertex.
      int outDegreeOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the out-degree of the specified vertex, which is the number of outgoing edges from the vertex.
      java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> outgoingEdgesOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the set of edges outgoing from the specified vertex.
      boolean removeAllEdges​(java.util.Collection<? extends Edge<V,​W>> edges)
      Removes all specified edges from the graph.
      java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> removeAllEdges​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Removes all edges connecting the specified vertices.
      boolean removeAllVertices​(java.util.Collection<? extends V> vertices)
      Removes all specified vertices and their incident edges from the graph.
      boolean removeEdge​(Edge<V,​W> e)
      Removes the specified edge from the graph.
      Edge<V,​W> removeEdge​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Removes the edge connecting the specified vertices.
      boolean removeVertex​(V v)
      Removes the specified vertex and all its edges from the graph.
      void setEdgeWeight​(Edge<V,​W> edge, double weight)
      Sets weight if W is of type Double, Integer, Float, Long, Short, Byte, otherwise throws exception
      void setEdgeWeight​(Edge<V,​W> edge, W weight)
      Sets edge weight
      java.lang.String toString()  
      java.util.Set<V> vertexSet()
      Returns the set of all vertices in the graph.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Graph

        public Graph()
        Constructor creates an empty adjacencyList

    • Method Detail

      • edgeSet

        public java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> edgeSet()
        Returns the set of all edges in the graph.
        Specified by:
        edgeSet in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        the set of all edges in the graph Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges.

      • edgesOf

        public java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> edgesOf​(V v)
        Returns the set of edges connected to the specified vertex.
        Specified by:
        edgesOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        v - the vertex whose edges are to be returned
        Returns:
        the set of edges connected to the specified vertex Time complexity: O(deg(v)), where deg(v) is the degree of the vertex. We require O(deg(v)) to copy the edges to the new HashSet. Lookup happens in O(1),

      • removeEdge

        public boolean removeEdge​(Edge<V,​W> e)
        Removes the specified edge from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        e - the edge to be removed
        Returns:
        true if the edge was successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(1) to lookup and remove the Edge using HashSet

      • removeVertex

        public boolean removeVertex​(V v)
        Removes the specified vertex and all its edges from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeVertex in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        v - the vertex to be removed
        Returns:
        true if the vertex was successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges.

      • vertexSet

        public java.util.Set<V> vertexSet()
        Returns the set of all vertices in the graph.
        Specified by:
        vertexSet in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        the set of all vertices in the graph Time complexity: O(V).

      • getEdge

        public Edge<V,​W> getEdge​(V vertex1,
                               V vertex2)
        Returns the edge connecting the specified vertices.
        Specified by:
        getEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the first vertex
        vertex2 - the second vertex
        Returns:
        the edge connecting the specified vertices, or null if no such edge exists Time complexity: O(deg(vertex1)), deg being the amount of edges connected to vertex1.

      • getAllEdges

        public java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> getAllEdges​(V vertex1,

        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the first vertex
        vertex2 - the second vertex
        Returns:
        a set of all edges connecting the specified vertices Time complexity: O(deg(vertex1)), deg being the amount of edges connected to vertex1.

      • containsEdge

        public boolean containsEdge​(V vertex1,
                            V vertex2)
        Checks if the graph contains an edge connecting the specified vertices.
        Specified by:
        containsEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the first vertex
        vertex2 - the second vertex
        Returns:
        true if the graph contains an edge connecting the specified vertices, false otherwise Time complexity: O(deg(vertex1)), deg(vertex1) being the number of edges connected to vertex1.

      • getEdgeWeight

        public double getEdgeWeight​(Edge<V,​W> edge)
        Returns the weight of the specified edge. If weight is null we return 0 and 1 if the weight cannot be cast to a Number type.
        Specified by:
        getEdgeWeight in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge whose weight is to be returned
        Returns:
        the weight of the specified edge Time complexity: O(1).

        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose in-degree is to be calculated
        Returns:

        Parameters:

        Returns:

        Specified by:

        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose out-degree is to be calculated
        Returns:

        Parameters:

        Returns:
        the set of edges outgoing from the specified vertex Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges.

      • removeAllEdges

        public boolean removeAllEdges​(java.util.Collection<? extends Edge<V,​W>> edges)
        Removes all specified edges from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeAllEdges in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edges - the edges to be removed
        Returns:
        true if any edges were successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges.

      • removeAllEdges

        public java.util.Set<Edge<V,​W>> removeAllEdges​(V vertex1,
                                                     V vertex2)
        Removes all edges connecting the specified vertices.
        Specified by:
        removeAllEdges in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the first vertex
        vertex2 - the second vertex
        Returns:
        the set of removed edges Time complexity: O(deg(vertex1)), where deg(vertex1) is the number of edges connected to vertex1.

      • removeAllVertices

        public boolean removeAllVertices​(java.util.Collection<? extends V> vertices)
        Removes all specified vertices and their incident edges from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeAllVertices in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertices - the vertices to be removed
        Returns:
        true if any vertices were successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(V + E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges.

      • removeEdge

        public Edge<V,​W> removeEdge​(V vertex1,
                                  V vertex2)
        Removes the edge connecting the specified vertices.
        Specified by:
        removeEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the first vertex
        vertex2 - the second vertex
        Returns:
        the removed edge, or null if no such edge existed Time complexity: O(deg(vertex1)), where deg(vertex1) is the number of edges connected to vertex1.

                          W weight)
        Sets edge weight
        Parameters:

        weight - to be assigned Time complexity: O(1)

        public void setEdgeWeight​(Edge<V,​W> edge,

        Sets weight if W is of type Double, Integer, Float, Long, Short, Byte, otherwise throws exception

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:

      • equals

        public boolean equals​(java.lang.Object obj)
        Compares this undirected graph to the specified object for equality.
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Parameters:
        obj - the object to compare with
        Returns:
        true if the graphs are equal, false otherwise Time complexity: O(V + E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges
